Q7 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

How may numbers can be made with digits 0, 7, 8 which are greater than ‘0’ and less than a million?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Number of ways to form one digit number = 2
‘0’ cannot be treated as one digit number
Number of ways to form two digit number = 2 × 3 = 6
Number of ways to form three digit number = 2 × 3 × 3 = 18
Number of ways to form four digit number = 2 × 33 = 54
Number of ways to form five digit number = 2 × 34 = 162
Number of ways to foron six digit number = 2 × 35 = 486
Total number of ways = 2 + 6 + 18 + 54 + 162 + 486 = 728

Q9 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Given below are four pairs of words. Identify the one which is different from others.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Murky as well as Dusky – both refer to something which is dark or cheerless – similar in meaning;
Inter means ‘to place’ and exhume means to dig out - opposite in meaning.
Effete means lacking in vigour whereas fructuous means productive or fertile – opposite in meaning.
Benediction is an utterance of good wishes while execration refers to denouncement i.e. both are
opposite in meaning.

Q18 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following is not the use of a symbol table in compiler design?

Correct answer: D, A, B, C
Solution

(d)
Both the analysis and synthesis components of a compiler use the symbol table.
(a) To keep all of the names of all entities in one place in a systematic format.
(b) To determine whether or not a variable has been declared.
(c) Type checking is implemented by ensuring that assignments and expressions in source code are semantically accurate.
(d) To figure out what a name’s scope is (scope resolution).

Q24 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following is/are correct with respect to Go-back-N and selective-repeat?

Correct answer: A, C
Solution

(a, c)
Go-back-N and selective-repeat can be seen as trade-offs between link bandwidth (data rate) and data link layer buffer space.
• If link bandwidth is large but buffer space is scarce, Go-back-N is preferred.
• If link bandwidth is small but buffer space is pretty, selective-repeat is preferred.

Q28 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are true?

Correct answer: B, C
Solution

(b, c)
(a) False, it must use a stable sorting algorithm.
(b) True, T.C. is ∉(n + k), here k = n.
(c) True, heap sort runs in ∉(n log n).
(d) False, the worst case unbalanced tree is a list, and balancing it requires ∧ (n) rotations.

Q29 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following relational instances:

 
Consider the following SQL queries Q1 and Q2.
Q1: SELECT DISTINCT E.Ename, E.age FROM Emp_info
Q2: SELECT E.Ename, E.age FROM Emp_info
Let X and Y be the number of tuples in the output of queries Q1 and Q2 respectively. Then (X2 – X1) is equal to ________.
NAT
Correct answer: 1
Solution

1
• Only 1 row in Emp_info is repeated twice.
• Distinct keyword will remove duplicates. Since there is only one duplicate, therefore (X2 – X1) will be equal to 1.
Hence 1 is the answer.

Q44 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, C
Solution

(b, c)
• Relative addressing uses a small offset instead of a full address. The offset usually requires
fewer bits than a full address. Therefore, instruction size does not increase; it may decrease or
remain same. So, option (a) is false
• The instruction contains an offset relative to PC.
Effective address calculation: EA = PC + offset.
Commonly used in branching to nearby instructions. So. Option (b) is true
• Instruction stores a pointer to a memory location. That memory location contains the actual
effective address of the operand. Useful for dynamic or changing operand locations.
So, option (c) is true.

Q46 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider following pseudo-codes for a process and TestAndSet function:

 
Assume N processes, where N ∈ 2, concurrently executing with function process shown above.
Which of the following is incorrect for critical section solution in above situation?
(Note: Initial value of Lock = FALSE)
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
If lock == FALSE, then all processes can go in critical section.
If lock == TRUE, then in next iteration of inner while lock = FALSE, and again all process can in
critical section. So, mutual exclusion not satisfy.
Any process can enter any time in critical section. So, solution satisfy progressive and bounded
waiting requirement.

Q50 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider relation schema R(P, Q, R, S) and the two sets of functional dependencies:
M = {P → Q, Q → P, PQ → R, PR → S, Q → R}
N = {RP → Q, QP → S, Q → S, SQ → R}
Which of the following is true?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Check N covers M:
1. P → Q cannot be derived from N which is present in M, so N not covers M.
Check M covers N:
1. RP → Q can be derived from M i.e. P → Q then RP → RQ.
2. QP → S can be derived from M i.e. P → Q, Q → PR, PR → S then Q → S and PQ → S.
3. Q → S can be derived from M i.e. P → Q, Q → PR, PR → S then Q → S.
4. SQ → R can be derived from M i.e. Q → R then SQ → SR.
Hence M covers N.

Q52 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following resource allocation graph of a system with P0, P1, P2 and P3 processes, and R0, R1, R2 and R3 resources.

 
 
Small circle in rectangle shows instance of that resource. So, R0, R1 and R3 have single instances and R2 has two instances. Consider the following statements for above resource allocation graph of system:
S1 : System is in deadlock.
S2 : Cycle in resource allocation graph is sufficient to detect deadlock in system with multiple instance for resources.
S3 : If P1 process from above system is aborted/terminated then system will be free from deadlock.
Which of above statements is/are correct?
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Cycle is necessary, but not sufficient condition for deadlock in multi-instance resource system.
In above graph, no process able to complete its execution without its required resource. So, it is in deadlock.
If P1 is aborted, then system will remain in deadlock state. Because, it does not hold any resource.
If any process other than P1 get aborted then system will be deadlock free.
